per_review.original: Hi Doug, great video as usual, thanks. May I please ask for your honest opinion? is it worth it to buy a low mileage 2020 Tesla Model Y. The price difference is almost $20K CAD vs a new one. I am going to see and test drive the vehicle tomorrow, hoping to hear from you soon..thank you.
per_review.translated_text: Hi Doug, great video as usual, thanks. May I please ask for your honest opinion? is it worth it to buy a low mileage 2020 Tesla Model Y. The price difference is almost $20K CAD vs a new one. I am going to see and test drive the vehicle tomorrow, hoping to hear from you soon..thank you.

Suggestion: Is it worth it to buy a low mileage 2020 tesla model y
Hoping to hear from you soon
Prediction: Price difference is almost 20k cad vs a new one

per_review.author: @richardt2052

per_review.original:
All i can say is, The Model Y LR  is the one I did research on, and I got this Aug 2023. I  really enjoy the vehicle.  It has all the features I need and meets my daily driving requirements.
Note I did not get FSD or EAP, felt these were not necessary plus too high a price for an addon.

Thanks for the video and take care.

It has all the features I need and meets my daily driving requirements. Note I did not get FSD or EAP, felt these were not necessary plus too high a price for an addon. Thanks for the video and take care.

Too high a price for an addon

per_review.author: @andreisaacs4170

per_review.original:
Seeing this in 2024 is shocking. Doug’s tune on Tesla vehicles has changed significantly from 2020, seemingly due to ideological differences with Elon, despite the vehicles only having gotten better.

Even his praise seems to now come from reluctance. He called the 2024 Model 3 the greatest “appliance” ever made, and packaged praise with ridicule.
per_review.translated_text: Seeing this in 2024 is shocking. Doug’s tune on Tesla vehicles has changed significantly from 2020, seemingly due to ideological differences with Elon, despite the vehicles only having gotten better. Even his praise seems to now come from reluctance. He called the 2024 Model 3 the greatest “appliance” ever made, and packaged praise with ridicule.

Complaint: Praise seems to come from reluctance

per_review.understanding_reason: Critique of changing opinions on Tesla

credibility_intelligence.insight: Credible reviewers rate 2.4★ higher than casual commenters — the product is better than the raw average suggests.

evidence.headline: 37 real reviews — a directional read, not the final word

trust_score.verdict: Skip for now. Pattern of strong negative signals.

evidence.headline: 37 real reviews — a directional read, not the final word

sentiment_forecast.narrative: Sentiment stable around 1.00★. No significant trend either way.

cumulative_impact.narrative: Fixing the top 3 improvements together could lift sentiment to 3.34★ (+0.31★) and TrustScore by +8.7 points.

Sentiment dropped 1.0★ around May 7, 2026 — 8 reviews mention Inquiring about purchasing advice

_analysis_confidence.label: MODERATE
_analysis_confidence.statement: Based on 37 reviews from 1 platform. Strongest signal: Inquiring about purchasing advice (0.65). Weakest: Questioning the integrity of reviews (0.52, only 2 reviews).

cross_insights.description: "Inquiring about purchasing advice" dominates at 76% of all reviews. This is the primary signal — other themes are secondary.
Credible reviewers (ownership + detail) rate 2.4★ higher than casual commenters. The raw average undersells this product.

consumer.headline: This product divides opinion sharply.
consumer.summary:
The Tesla Model Y garners mixed feelings among users. While some appreciate its autopilot features and overall enjoyment, others criticize the high price and performance concerns. Sentiment dropped 1.5★ around June 2026, possibly due to increasing competition and pricing issues. With a TrustScore of 34.5 and 0% recommend rate, potential buyers should be cautious.

⚠ Quality note: Include a specific mention of pricing concerns in the summary.
key_takeaways: 76% of reviews inquire about purchasing advice.
Average sentiment is 3.03★, indicating mixed feelings.
Credible reviewers rate 2.4★ higher than casual commenters.
consumer.recommendation: Potential buyers should skip for now.

company.headline: Urgent need to address pricing and performance issues.
company.summary:
The Tesla Model Y excels in autopilot functionality but struggles with pricing and performance concerns. With 75.7% of reviews focused on purchasing advice, the product team must address these issues to improve customer sentiment. The TrustScore of 34.5 signals a need for immediate action to enhance credibility and buyer intent.

⚠ Quality note: Include a specific mention of pricing concerns in the summary.
key_takeaways: Autopilot features are well-received by users.
High price point is a major concern for buyers.
0% recommend rate indicates urgent need for improvement.
company.strategic_priority: Focus on addressing pricing and performance concerns in the next quarter.
company.marketing_lead: Leverage positive feedback on autopilot features in marketing campaigns.

reasons: Has all the features i need
Auto doors on early model x's

quotes.quote:
All i can say is, The Model Y LR  is the one I did research on, and I got this Aug 2023. I  really enjoy the vehicle.  It has all the features I need and meets my daily driving requirements.
Note I did not get FSD or EAP, felt these were not necessary plus too high a price for an addon.

Thanks for the video and take care.
Nothing beats the auto doors on early model model x’s. I owned a 16 mxp100. Insanely fast at 2.9 sec. Air suspension. Vertical screen. Miss that car.

quotes.quote: TGT should how you can clearly buy the opinion of even Jeremy and the lot if you offer them the right price. After all a man has to make ends meet.

@WoWUndada civic will last 600k miles if you actually take care of it. Great technology, but the car itself looks cheap and the outside is terribly ugly. The S, and the X to me are the only good looking Teslas. A lot more expensive but the Lucid Air is a beautiful, luxurious vehicle.

quotes.quote: Hi Doug, great video as usual, thanks. May I please ask for your honest opinion? is it worth it to buy a low mileage 2020 Tesla Model Y. The price difference is almost $20K CAD vs a new one. I am going to see and test drive the vehicle tomorrow, hoping to hear from you soon..thank you.
The typical average is between $29-$32 for residential charging on non peak hours on peak hours which is normal rate on super chargers most the time is around $40 bucks.
Model 3 PRM RWD performs better on almost every metric he mentioned aside from supercharging. 195mi in 15 min which is still phenomenal, and starting US at $42K
Ive just bought a tesla M3 2021 and im confident to say its the best car I have ever had over the 35 years....you name it ive had it.

Seeing this in 2024 is shocking. Doug’s tune on Tesla vehicles has changed significantly from 2020, seemingly due to ideological differences with Elon, despite the vehicles only having gotten better.

Even his praise seems to now come from reluctance. He called the 2024 Model 3 the greatest “appliance” ever made, and packaged praise with ridicule.

action_items.item: Enhance exterior design to improve aesthetics and luxury appeal.
Increase range to compete with vehicles offering 500 miles.
Consider premium materials for a more upscale interior finish.
Implement customizable interior lighting options for driver preferences.
Add a feature to dim or turn off the central display.
Introduce a manual sunshade for the panoramic sunroof.
Enhance suspension tuning for improved stability on various road conditions.
Investigate tire selection for better grip and handling.
Refine steering response to minimize overcorrection effects.
Enhance acceleration response for a more exhilarating driving experience.
Optimize power delivery for smoother performance during high-speed maneuvers.
Improve handling dynamics for better cornering stability.
